one_line_takeaway: GeoTexPuri trains classifiers to fuse adversarial images with a normalized Signed-Distance-Field rendering of the object mask (via a three-stream CE loss), yielding a deterministic, inference-time-free adversarial defense that the paper reports beats diffusion-based purification (OSCP, DiffPure, GDMP) and adversarial-training baselines (MeanSparse, Singh et al.) on ImageNet clean/robust accuracy under PGD and AutoAttack, at a fraction of the training/inference compute cost, though without any adaptive-attack evaluation.
key_finding: Under AutoAttack with a ConvNeXt-L backbone, GeoTexPuri reaches 84.79% clean accuracy and 83.52% robust accuracy, which the paper reports as a 6.83-point clean-accuracy gain (84.79% vs. 77.96% for MeanSparse) and a 9.33-point robust-accuracy gain (83.52% vs. 74.19% for OSCP, ResNet-50) over the strongest baselines in that comparison row. Under untargeted PGD-100 (ResNet-50), GeoTexPuri gets 77.57% clean / 74.47% robust vs. OSCP's 77.63% clean / 73.89% robust and GDMP's 73.53%/72.97%; with ConvNeXt-L it reaches 82.87%/81.51%. Under targeted PGD-40 (ResNet-152), GeoTexPuri gets 81.55% clean / 80.90% robust vs. OSCP's 79.81%/78.78% and GDMP's 78.10%/77.86%; with ConvNeXt-L it reaches 84.31%/83.38%. The paper also reports large compute-cost reductions versus the diffusion-distillation baseline OSCP (Table 1): e.g., AutoAttack setting, ResNet-50, training drops from 93h to 19.5h and inference from 2h to 35 minutes (OSCP figures on 4 GPUs vs. GeoTexPuri on a single H100). Ablations (Table 3) show SDF outperforms edge/contour/skeleton geometric encodings at matched settings (e.g. AutoAttack: SDF 84.79/83.52 vs. skeleton 84.53/83.13 vs. contour 84.50/82.84 vs. edge 83.77/82.01), and that the framework is fairly mask-quality-robust: Otsu-thresholded masks (79.47/76.43 clean/robust, untargeted PGD-100) trail the DINO+HQ-SAM masks (80.00/77.35) by only 0.92 points, and InSPyReNet masks (79.73/77.13) sit in between. Beta=0.5 is optimal for untargeted/AutoAttack; beta=0.7 is optimal for targeted PGD-40 (81.82/81.69), with performance varying only ~1-2 points across beta in [0.3,0.7].
core_contribution: The paper's OWN contribution is GeoTexPuri (also titled in the PDF body 'Geometric and Texture balancing Purification'; the KB record's title 'ShapePuri' does not match the extracted PDF, which is titled 'Enhancing Adversarial Robustness with Signed Distance Fields for Harmonizing Geometric Invariance and Texture'). GeoTexPuri is a training-time adversarial-robustness framework (not an inference-time purifier like diffusion-based purification) that fuses a Signed Distance Field (SDF), computed from an object segmentation mask, into the adversarial training image so the classifier internalizes geometry-anchored, texture-preserving representations. A pretrained mask is obtained offline via Grounding DINO (open-vocabulary detection for the object's bounding box) plus HQ-SAM (box-prompted high-quality segmentation) to produce a binary object mask; the SDF is then computed from this mask via inner/outer Euclidean Distance Transforms (Eq. 2: I_SDF = DT(I_mask) - DT(1-I_mask)), normalized to [-1,+1]. The SDF is fused with the adversarial image as I_fusion = I_adv ⊙ (1 + β·I_SDF) (Eq. 3, β=0.5 default). Training uses a three-stream cross-entropy objective L_total = CE(clean) + CE(adversarial) + CE(fusion) (Eq. 4, Algorithm 1). At inference time the model runs as a standard classifier with no auxiliary geometric module or extra compute -- the SDF/mask machinery is training-only.
threat_model: White-box, Lp-bounded evasion attacks against an image classifier at test/inference time. Specifically: untargeted PGD-100 with L_inf epsilon=4/255, step size 1/255; targeted PGD-40 with L_inf epsilon=16/255, step size 0.4/255; and AutoAttack (standard ensemble of APGD-CE, APGD-DLR, FAB, Square) with epsilon=4/255. The paper does not evaluate adaptive attacks that are aware of the SDF-fusion training mechanism, nor does it discuss gradient-obfuscation/BPDA-style adaptive-attack risk that is a known concern for purification-style defenses -- this is a gap in the paper's own evaluation, not something it claims to address.
limitations: Explicitly paper-stated or paper-evident limitations: (1) The pipeline depends on an offline mask-precomputation stage (Grounding DINO + HQ-SAM) that the paper itself reports costs approximately 48 hours, even though this is excluded from the reported training-time figures. (2) No adaptive-attack evaluation is presented -- all attacks (PGD, AutoAttack) are evaluated against the undefended-gradient assumption typical of standard benchmarks, without testing an attacker aware of the SDF-fusion training scheme or attempting gradient approximation/BPDA against it, which is a known weak spot for purification-adjacent defenses. (3) The document itself contains leftover LaTeX-template placeholder text ('F. Author et al.' as the running header on multiple pages), indicating this PDF is an unfinished/anonymized submission draft rather than a finalized camera-ready version, which is a grounding caveat for anyone treating exact page/table numbering as final. (4) The KB record's title ('ShapePuri...') does not match the paper's actual title as extracted from the PDF ('Enhancing Adversarial Robustness with Signed Distance Fields for Harmonizing Geometric Invariance and Texture' / in-text name 'GeoTexPuri'), suggesting a metadata mismatch upstream of this review that should be corrected in the KB. (5) Table 5 (full ImageNet-1k) reports an AutoAttack robust accuracy (79.83%) exceeding its own clean accuracy (78.65%) for the ResNet-101 configuration, and CelebA-HQ Table 6 similarly shows robust accuracy exceeding clean accuracy in two of three rows (e.g., 89.51% clean vs. 92.74% robust at epsilon=4/255,10 iters); the paper does not explain this counter-intuitive pattern, so it is reported here as-is rather than reconciled.

datasets_used: ImageNet validation-set split: 40,000 images for training / 10,000 for testing, resized to 512x512 (main experiments, following the OSCP protocol)
Full ImageNet-1k: 1,281,167 training images / 50,000 validation images, 1,000 categories
CelebA-HQ: 28,299 images (23,533 train / 4,766 test), with CelebAMask-HQ facial-component masks used to build unified masks for SDF computation
quantitative_results: AutoAttack, ConvNeXt-L, GeoTexPuri: 84.79% clean / 83.52% robust accuracy

AutoAttack, ResNet-50, GeoTexPuri: 78.25% clean / 75.47% robust vs. OSCP (ResNet-50) 77.63%/74.19%, DiffPure (ResNet-50) 75.77%/73.02%, MeanSparse (ConvNeXt-L) 77.96%/59.64%, Singh et al. (ConvNeXt-L) 77.00%/57.70%, and 'Without defense' (ResNet-50) 80.55%/0.00%
Untargeted PGD-100, ResNet-50, GeoTexPuri: 77.57% clean / 74.47% robust vs. OSCP 77.63%/73.89%, GDMP 73.53%/72.97%, 'Without defense' 80.55%/0.01%; GeoTexPuri ConvNeXt-L: 82.87%/81.51%
Targeted PGD-40, ResNet-152, GeoTexPuri: 81.55% clean / 80.90% robust vs. OSCP 79.81%/78.78%, GDMP 78.10%/77.86%, 'Without defense' 82.33%/0.04%; GeoTexPuri ConvNeXt-L: 84.31%/83.38%
Compute cost (Table 1), AutoAttack setting: OSCP (ResNet-50, 4 GPUs) trains in 93h and infers in 2h; GeoTexPuri (ResNet-50, single H100) trains in 19.5h and infers in 35 minutes
Compute cost, untargeted setting: OSCP (ResNet-50) 71h train/3h test vs GeoTexPuri (ResNet-50) 16.3h train/50m test; targeted setting: OSCP (ResNet-152) 65h/3h vs GeoTexPuri (ResNet-152) 22.8h/40m
Ablation - geometric representation (AutoAttack, ConvNeXt-L): Edge 83.77%/82.01%, Contour 84.50%/82.84%, Skeleton 84.53%/83.13%, SDF (proposed) 84.79%/83.52%
Ablation - mask generator (untargeted PGD-100): Otsu thresholding 79.47%/76.43%, InSPyReNet 79.73%/77.13%, Grounding DINO+HQ-SAM (proposed) 80.00%/77.35%

Full ImageNet-1k (Table 5): targeted PGD-40 (ResNet-152 config) 81.39% clean / 80.54% robust; AutoAttack (ResNet-101, substituted for ConvNeXt-L due to compute) 78.65% clean / 79.83% robust; untargeted PGD-100 (ResNet-101) 79.80% clean / 79.72% robust

CelebA-HQ (Table 6, ArcFace backbone, targeted attacks): epsilon=4/255,10 iters,step 0.25/255 -> 89.51% clean/92.74% robust; epsilon=4/255,100 iters,step 1/255 -> 86.43%/87.89%; epsilon=16/255,40 iters,step 0.4/255 -> 88.48%/95.80%
baselines_compared: GDMP (guided diffusion model for adversarial purification)
OSCP (instant adversarial purification with adversarial consistency distillation)
Singh et al. (adversarial training revisited for ImageNet, architectures/training/generalization)
Otsu thresholding + connected-component mask generator (ablation baseline)
Canny edge / contour / skeleton geometric encodings (ablation baselines vs. SDF)
